title: Apparatus for sterilizing air from breathalyzer
abstract: The present disclosure relates to an apparatus (100) for sterilizing air, the apparatus includes testing device (102) configured in a housing, an inlet of the testing device receives air exhaled by a user (202). An isolation chamber (104) collects air exhaled by the user. Chambers comprising a first chamber (106) adapted to receive air collected in the isolation chamber to filter out a first set of contaminants from the air, a second chamber (108) coupled to the first chamber to filter out a second set of contaminants, a third chamber (110) coupled to the second chamber, to filter out a third set of contaminants; and a fourth chamber (112) coupled to the third chamber, to filter out a fourth set of contaminants, wherein the filtered air from corresponding chambers is released to premises.
claims: Claims : An apparatus (100) for sterilizing air, said apparatus comprising: a testing device (102) configured in a housing of the apparatus, an inlet of said testing device (102) adapted to receive air exhaled by a user, the housing located in a premises; an insolation chamber (104) coupled to the testing device (102), the insolation chamber collects air exhaled by the user, one or more separators defining one or more chambers, the one or more separators form a seal between the one or more chambers, the one or more chambers collects air from the insolation chamber (104) through negative pressure, the one or more chambers comprising: a first chamber (106) adapted to receive air collected in the isolation chamber (104), the first chamber (106) comprising a first filtering element and fluidically coupled to the isolation chamber (104), the first chamber (106) adapted to allow air from the isolation chamber to flow through the first filtering element in a first direction, wherein the first filtering element is adapted to filter out a first set of contaminants from the air; a second chamber (108) comprising a second filtering element and fluidically coupled to the first chamber (106), the second chamber (108) adapted to allow air from the first chamber (106) to flow through the second filtering element in a second direction, wherein the second filtering element is adapted to filter out a second set of contaminants from the air; a third chamber (110) comprising a third filtering element and fluidically coupled to the second chamber (108), the third chamber (110) adapted to allow air from the second chamber (108) to flow through the third filtering element in a third direction, wherein the third filtering element is adapted to filter out a third set of contaminants from the air; and a fourth chamber (112) comprising a fourth filtering element and fluidically coupled to the third chamber (110), the fourth chamber (112) adapted to allow air from the third chamber to flow through the fourth filtering element in a fourth direction, the fourth filtering element is adapted to filter out a fourth set of contaminants from the air, wherein the filtered air from corresponding chambers is released to the premises. 2. The apparatus as claimed in claim 1, wherein the first filtering element in the first chamber (106) is a high efficiency particulate air (HEPA) filter and wherein the second filtering element in the second chamber (108) is aH14 filter. 3. The apparatus as claimed in claim 1, wherein the flow of air is maintained by vacuum/suction fan (124) maintaining negative pressure. 4. The apparatus as claimed in claim 1, wherein the third filtering/sterilizing element in the third chamber (110) is a plasma gas. 5. The apparatus as claimed in claim 1, wherein the fourth filtering/sterilizing element in the fourth chamber (112) is quaternary ammonium compound. 6. The apparatus as claimed in claim 1, wherein the apparatus comprises an air outlet (114)that receives the filtered air from the fourth chamber, wherein the air outlet (114) comprises a fifth filtering element(122) to filter the air received from the fourth chamber, the fifth filtering element is an activated charcoal. 7. The apparatus as claimed in claim 1, wherein the apparatus comprises user interface (116) located in an outer portion of the housing for easy access of options, wherein the apparatus (100) is connected to audio visual (AV) output, printer and any combination thereof. 8. The apparatus as claimed in claim 1, wherein the first set of contaminates are airborne particles from the air and second set of contaminates are submicron size particles from the air. 9. The apparatus as claimed in claim 1, wherein the third set of contaminates are microbes in the air. 10. The apparatus as claimed in claim 1, wherein fourth set of contaminates are microorganisms including bacteria, viruses and prions.
